description: Easily broken or damaged; requiring careful handling. 'Welua' names the quality of fragility—the delicacy that calls for gentleness, the vulnerability that demands care.
sound_symbolism: The word asks for care. 'We' opens with gentle awareness, delicacy present. 'Lu' moves with careful flow, caution maintained. Finally 'a' opens to vulnerability, fragility acknowledged.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'welua lopha' (fragile vessel). Related to 'phaeli' (tender) but emphasizes susceptibility to damage rather than softness of quality.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Recognizing fragility awakens compassion—seeing how easily things break opens the heart to protection.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fragile things taught care—handling delicate objects developed gentleness and attention.
